summary: Matched and linked the Game & Watch greenhouse item source by moving itgamewatchgreenhouse.c from NonMatching to Matching, replacing address-style symbols with item-specific names, exporting the greenhouse API through itgamewatchgreenhouse.h, and updating Game & Watch Attack11 and the item logic table to use those names. The matching work also factored repeated owner/check logic into a small inline helper and used explicit stack padding plus local sdata2 symbol scoping to preserve the match.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so melee/it/items/itgamewatchgreenhouse.c is built as Object(Matching) instead of Object(NonMatching).

Renamed greenhouse symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt from generic address or logic-table names such as it_802C61F4, it_2725_Logic71_Destroyed, and itGamewatchgreenhouse_UnkMotion3_Anim to item-scoped names such as itGamewatchGreenhouse_Spawn, itGamewatchGreenhouse_Destroyed, and itGamewatchGreenhouse_Motion3_Anim.
Changed the sdata2 symbol at 0x804DD290 from global it_804DD290 to local @148, indicating the float belonged to compiler-generated/local constant usage rather than a meaningful exported object.
Moved greenhouse public prototypes out of the broad it_27CF.h and it_27CF.dox declarations and into src/melee/it/items/itgamewatchgreenhouse.h.
Updated ftGw_Attack11.c to include itgamewatchgreenhouse.h instead of it_27CF.h and to call the renamed greenhouse spawn, remove, hitlag, and motion-selection functions.
Updated the Game & Watch greenhouse entry in it_803F3100 in it_2725.c to use itGamewatchGreenhouse_Destroyed, itGamewatchGreenhouse_PickedUp, and itGamewatchGreenhouse_EvtUnk.
Defined ItemStateTable it_803F7898 directly in itgamewatchgreenhouse.c with motion callbacks for states 0 through 3.
Refactored repeated item owner validation and ftGw_Attack11_ItemGreenhouse_CheckAll calls into static inline greenhouse_Check.
Reworked destruction/removal flow so itGamewatchGreenhouse_802C6328 delegates flag-setting behavior through itGamewatchGreenhouse_Destroyed before calling Item_8026A8EC.
Added forward/header includes needed by the matched source and header, including it/forward.h, it/items/forward.h, baselib/forward.h, dolphin/mtx.h, and string.h.
smart_moves: Renaming the functions around the concrete item name made cross-file callsites readable while preserving unknown suffixes like 802C6328 for behavior that was not identified from the evidence.
Keeping partially understood functions as itGamewatchGreenhouse_802C6430-style names avoided overclaiming semantics while still improving namespace ownership over raw it_802Cxxxx names.
Moving prototypes from the catch-all it_27CF header into itgamewatchgreenhouse.h reduced header leakage and made ftGw_Attack11.c depend on the specific item interface it actually uses.

Factoring the repeated owner/check condition into greenhouse_Check reduced duplicate control flow in Motion2_Anim and Motion3_Anim, apparently while still preserving the expected assembly via inline use and PAD_STACK annotations.
Changing the item logic table to use greenhouse-specific names aligned the central logic dispatch with the matched item source and symbols.
Marking the file Matching in configure.py only after symbol, header, and source cleanup ensured the build/link configuration reflected an actually matched translation unit.
decomp_lessons: When matching an item file, update all three surfaces together: build classification in configure.py, symbols.txt names/scopes, and all cross-file prototypes/callsites.
For item-specific APIs that were temporarily declared in broad numbered headers, migrate them into the item’s own header once the file is understood enough to link cleanly.
Use a concrete item prefix, here itGamewatchGreenhouse_, for functions tied to a specific item even when the exact behavior is still unknown.
It is acceptable to preserve address suffixes under a meaningful namespace for unidentified helpers rather than inventing semantic names.
Repeated source simplification can still be match-safe if the compiler behavior is controlled with static inline helpers and explicit PAD_STACK where required.
Logic table callback names should be synchronized with source and symbol names so future readers can connect item IDs, dispatch behavior, and implementation files quickly.
Local constants in symbols.txt should be demoted from invented global names to local @ labels when matching reveals they are not meaningful exported data.
naming_conventions: Use CamelCase item namespace prefix for this item: itGamewatchGreenhouse_.
Rename spawn entrypoint from raw address name it_802C61F4 to semantic itGamewatchGreenhouse_Spawn.
Rename lifecycle callbacks from it_2725_Logic71_Destroyed/PickedUp/EvtUnk to item-scoped itGamewatchGreenhouse_Destroyed/PickedUp/EvtUnk.

Rename animation callbacks from itGamewatchgreenhouse_UnkMotion*_Anim to itGamewatchGreenhouse_Motion*_Anim, correcting capitalization and dropping the weak Unk prefix where motion index is sufficient.
For unidentified item helpers, prefer itGamewatchGreenhouse_802Cxxxx over raw it_802Cxxxx to show ownership without guessing behavior.
Use item-specific headers named after the item source, such as itgamewatchgreenhouse.h, for item APIs used by fighter code.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(8) in itGamewatchGreenhouse_Motion3_Anim and PAD_STACK(0x10) in itGamewatchGreenhouse_Motion2_Anim to maintain stack layout for matching.
Introduced static inline greenhouse_Check to share repeated control flow while likely allowing the compiler to emit match-compatible code.
Kept a local Item* declaration in motion functions even where the excerpt does not show later direct use, suggesting variable/layout preservation for matching.
Changed Item_80268E5C((HSD_GObj*) item_gobj, 2, ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE) to Item_80268E5C(item_gobj, 2, ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE) in Motion2_Anim, relying on compatible typedefs/casts while preserving behavior.
Adjusted the 0x804DD290 sdata2 symbol to local @148 scope, a typical symbol-map cleanup when a constant is compiler-local rather than a named global.
Preserved exact dispatch state indices 0, 1, 2, and 3 in it_803F7898, mapping them to Motion3_Anim or Motion2_Anim callbacks.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The only visible activity evidence is the merged PR diff; there is no explicit reviewer rationale or requested change to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename itGamewatchGreenhouse_802C6328 or the other 802Cxxxx greenhouse helpers to semantic names.
Search for similar Game & Watch item PRs that migrate prototypes out of it_27CF.h into item-specific headers.
Compare itgamewatchmanhole, itgamewatchfire, and itgamewatchparachute matching tactics with the greenhouse static inline/PAD_STACK approach.
Audit remaining it_27CF.h declarations for item-specific functions that should move into dedicated item headers.
Look for other symbol-map changes where global it_804Dxxxx sdata2 constants were converted to local @ labels during matching.
